slides.headline: The brief said:
slides.eyebrow: Behind the Scenes · Anatomy of a sync brief

slides.body: "90 seconds. Orchestral. Afrofusion influence. Hopeful. No vocals. One-stop only."
Every word in a sync brief is a specification. Not a suggestion. ↓

⏱ 90 SECONDS
The edit is locked. Not "around 90 seconds." Exactly 90 seconds. Match it.

🎻 ORCHESTRAL
Real strings or a convincing orchestral arrangement. Bedroom synth pads don't make the shortlist here.

🌍 AFROFUSION INFLUENCE
The tonal and rhythmic language of African music — not pastiche, not performed. Authentic. 😌 HOPEFUL
Not happy. Not triumphant. Hopeful is quieter, more complex. The distinction matters enormously to the picture edit. 🚫 NO VOCALS
Not even background vocals. Not even a hum. Fully instrumental or the track is out. ✅ ONE-STOP ONLY
You control both master and publishing. If you need a label involved, the track is disqualified. → Where sync briefs come from and who writes them
→ Why duration is always absolute
→ How to read instrumentation and influence language
→ Mood vocabulary — the most misread field in any brief
→ Why "no vocals" is never negotiable
→ How to know if you qualify for one-stop before applying Save this for when your first brief arrives. #SyncBrief #MusicSupervisor #SyncLicensing #AfricanComposers #SyncMaster
